gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/253)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/253)
text: شَارِبُ الْخَمْرِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَأْتِي مُسْوَدّاً وَجْهُهُ مَائِلًا شِقُّهُ مُدْلَعاً لِسَانُهُ يُنَادِي الْعَطَشَ الْعَطَشَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The wine drinker would come on the Day of Judgement as blackened of face, bent side, drooping tongue, calling out, ‘The thirst! The thirst!’
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Fadl from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘On the Day of Judgment, the wine drinker will come with a black face, with his one-half bent and his tongue hanging out crying, “Thirsty, thirsty.’””
